title: ifloga spicata subsp. hadidii
intro: Ifloga spicata subsp. hadidii, also known as Hadidi's ifloga, is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family and is native to Ethiopia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antimalarial activities. The health benefits of Hadidi's ifloga include the treatment of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and stems are typically dried, crushed, and infused in hot water or mixed with other herbs to create teas, infusions, or topical applications.
